{"code": "# Build the caller workflows and push them to repos via GH API
import subprocess, json, base64

def update_file(repo, path, content, commit_msg):
    """Create or update a file in a repo using gh api."""
    # Encode content to base64
    b64 = base64.b64encode(content.encode()).decode()
    
    # Check if file exists (get sha)
    r = subprocess.run(
        ["gh", "api", f"repos/Ai-Whisperers/{repo}/contents/{path}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
    )
    sha = None
    if r.returncode == 0:
        try:
            sha = json.loads(r.stdout)["sha"]
        except:
            pass
    
    # Build payload
    payload = {
        "message": commit_msg,
        "content": b64,
    }
    if sha:
        payload["sha"] = sha
    
    r2 = subprocess.run(
        ["gh", "api", f"repos/Ai-Whisperers/{repo}/contents/{path}",
         "--method", "PUT",
         "-f", f"message={commit_msg}",
         "-f", f"content={b64}"] + 
        (["-f", f"sha={sha}"] if sha else []),
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=20
    )
    return r2.status_code, r2.stdout[:200] if r2.stdout else r2.stderr[:200]

# Build standard deploy.yml caller
def build_deploy_caller(site_name, site_url=None, pm="npm", node="20", ci=True):
    lines = []
    lines.append("name: Deploy")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("on:")
    lines.append("  push:")
    lines.append("    branches: [main]")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("jobs:")
    if ci:
        lines.append("  ci:")
        lines.append("    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/ci-nextjs.yml@main")
        lines.append("    with:")
        lines.append("      site-url: '%s'" % (site_url or "http://localhost:3000"))
        lines.append("      package-manager: '%s'" % pm)
        lines.append("      node-version: '%s'" % node)
        lines.append("    secrets: inherit")
        lines.append("")
    lines.append("  deploy:")
    if ci:
        lines.append("    needs: ci")
    lines.append("    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/deploy-vps.yml@main")
    lines.append("    with:")
    lines.append("      site-name: '%s'" % site_name)
    lines.append("      package-manager: '%s'" % pm)
    lines.append("      node-version: '%s'" % node)
    lines.append("    secrets: inherit")
    return "\n".join(lines)

# Build git-pull deploy caller
def build_git_pull_caller(site_name, vps_dir):
    lines = []
    lines.append("name: Deploy")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("on:")
    lines.append("  push:")
    lines.append("    branches: [main]")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("jobs:")
    lines.append("  deploy:")
    lines.append("    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/deploy-vps-git-pull.yml@main")
    lines.append("    with:")
    lines.append("      site-name: '%s'" % site_name)
    lines.append("      vps-dir: '%s'" % vps_dir)
    lines.append("    secrets: inherit")
    return "\n".join(lines)

# Build CI-only caller
def build_ci_caller(site_url=None, pm="npm", node="20"):
    lines = []
    lines.append("name: CI")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("on:")
    lines.append("  push:")
    lines.append("    branches: [main]")
    lines.append("  pull_request:")
    lines.append("    branches: [main]")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("jobs:")
    lines.append("  ci:")
    lines.append("    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/ci-nextjs.yml@main")
    lines.append("    with:")
    lines.append("      site-url: '%s'" % (site_url or "http://localhost:3000"))
    lines.append("      package-manager: '%s'" % pm)
    lines.append("      node-version: '%s'" % node)
    lines.append("    secrets: inherit")
    return "\n".join(lines)

results = []

# 1. fun4me — replace deploy.yml

{"code": "import subprocess, json, base64

def update_file(repo, path, content, commit_msg):
    b64 = base64.b64encode(content.encode()).decode()
    r = subprocess.run(
        ["gh", "api", f"repos/Ai-Whisperers/{repo}/contents/{path}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
    )
    sha = None
    if r.returncode == 0:
        try:
            sha = json.loads(r.stdout)["sha"]
        except:
            pass
    params = ["gh", "api", f"repos/Ai-Whisperers/{repo}/contents/{path}",
              "--method", "PUT",
              "-f", f"message={commit_msg}",
              "-f", f"content={b64}"]
    if sha:
        params += ["-f", f"sha={sha}"]
    r2 = subprocess.run(params, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=20)
    status = r2.returncode
    out = (r2.stdout or r2.stderr or "")[:120]
    return status, out

# Build callers
def deploy_caller(site, url, pm="npm", node="20", needs_ci=True):
    return f"""name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  ci:
    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/ci-nextjs.yml@main
    with:
      site-url: '{url}'
      package-manager: '{pm}'
      node-version: '{node}'
    secrets: inherit
  deploy:
    needs: ci
    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/deploy-vps.yml@main
    with:
      site-name: '{site}'
      package-manager: '{pm}'
      node-version: '{node}'
    secrets: inherit
"""

def git_pull_caller(site, vps_dir):
    return f"""name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/deploy-vps-git-pull.yml@main
    with:
      site-name: '{site}'
      vps-dir: '{vps_dir}'
    secrets: inherit
"""

def ci_caller(url, pm="npm", node="20"):
    return f"""name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  ci:
    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/ci-nextjs.yml@main
    with:
      site-url: '{url}'
      package-manager: '{pm}'
      node-version: '{node}'
    secrets: inherit
"""

results = []
targets = [
    ("fun4me", "deploy.yml", deploy_caller("fun4me", "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com", "pnpm", "22")),
    ("dayah-litworks", "deploy.yml", deploy_caller("dayah", "https://dayah.paragu-ai.com")),
    ("maiyu-atelier", "deploy.yml", deploy_caller("maiyu", "https://maiyu.paragu-ai.com")),
    ("villamayor-asociados", "deploy.yml", deploy_caller("villamayor", "https://villamayor.paragu-ai.com")),
    ("3md-website", "deploy.yml", deploy_caller("3md", "https://3md.paragu-ai.com")),
    ("template-nextjs-client", "deploy.yml", deploy_caller("template-nextjs-client", "http://localhost:3000")),
    ("elviajero-comercio", "deploy.yml", git_pull_caller("elviajero-comercio", "/tmp/elviajero-comercio")),
    ("ozmontania-website", "deploy.yml", git_pull_caller("ozmontania", "/root/ozmontania-website")),
]

# Add CI to repos that have no CI
ci_targets = [
    ("elviajero-comercio", "https://viajero.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("dayah-litworks", "https://dayah.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("maiyu-atelier", "https://maiyu.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("villamayor-asociados", "https://villamayor.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("3md-website", "https://3md.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("ozmontania-website", "https://ozmontania.paragu-ai.com"),
    ("template-nextjs-client", None),
]

for repo, path, content in targets:
    status, msg = update_file(repo, f".github/workflows/{path}", content, "refactor: use centralized ci-cd workflows")
    results.append((f"{repo}/{path}", status, msg))

# Add CI files to repos that don't have them
for repo, url in ci_targets:
    url_val = url or "http://localhost:3000"
    # Check if already has ci.yml
    r = subprocess.run(["gh", "api", f"repos/Ai-Whisperers/{repo}/contents/.github/workflows/ci.yml"],
                       capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
    if r.returncode == 0:
        continue
    content = ci_caller(url_val)
    status, msg = update_file(repo, ".github/workflows/ci.yml", content, "add: CI via centralized ci-cd workflows")
    results.append((f"{repo}/ci.yml", status, msg))

print("Results:")
for name, status, msg in results:
    ok = "OK" if status == 0 else "FAIL"
    print(f"  {name:40s} {ok} ({status}): {msg[:80]}")
"}

**Skills**: how to do this class of task. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the skill library: CLASS-LEVEL skills with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries.

Signals that warrant a skill update (any one is enough):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, verbosity, or approach. Frustration is a FIRST-CLASS skill signal, not just a memory signal. 'stop doing X', 'don't format like this', 'I hate when you Y' — embed the lesson in the skill that governs that task so the next session starts fixed.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, or debugging path emerged.
  • A skill that was loaded or consulted turned out wrong, missing, or outdated — patch it now.

Preference order for skills — pick the earliest that fits:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Check what skills were loaded via /skill-name or skill_view in the conversation. If one of them covers the learning, PATCH it first. It was in play; it's the right place.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (skills_list + skill_view to find the right one). Patch it.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella via skill_manage action=write_file. Three kinds: `references/<topic>.md` for session-specific detail OR condensed knowledge banks (quoted research, API docs excerpts, domain notes) written concise and task-focused; `templates/<name>.<ext>` for starter files meant to be copied and modified; `scripts/<name>.<ext>` for statically re-runnable actions (verification, fixture generators, probes). Add a one-line pointer in SKILL.md so future agents find them.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA when nothing exists. Name at the class level — NOT a PR number, error string, codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y' session artifact. If the name only fits today's task, fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding: when the user complains about how you handled a task, update the skill that governs that task — memory alone isn't enough. Memory says 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills say 'how to do this class of task for this user'. Both should carry user-preference lessons when relevant.

If you notice overlapping existing skills, mention it — the background curator handles consolidation.

Act on whichever of the two dimensions has real signal. If genuinely nothing stands out on either, say 'Nothing to save.' and stop — but don't reach for that conclusion as a default.
Good catch. Let me review the conversation for both dimensions.
